comments.author: Masai
comments.snippet: Product Management & Agentic AI program / starting over
comments.comment: Masai — The "starting over" line hits — agentic AI is resetting the PM skill bar faster than most programs admit. What surprised your team most in Haris's first 30 days: discovery depth or shipping with AI tooling?

comments.author: InterviewStack.io
comments.snippet: PM skills 2026: strategy 65.8%, roadmapping 60%, co-mentioned 45.9%
comments.comment: InterviewStack.io — The 46% co-mention of strategy + roadmapping matches what I see in live PM loops: teams hire for roadmaps but promote people who write the decision, not just the deck. Are you seeing strategy show up more as a JD keyword or an interview bar?

comments.author: Product Coalition
comments.snippet: AI as teammate in PM — LinkedIn Live with Dave West
comments.comment: Product Coalition — Framing AI as a teammate (not autopilot) for PMs/POs is the right tension. The failure mode I see: teams skip the written decision when the model drafts the roadmap story. What guardrail will Dave push on human accountability in the live?

comments.author: Awais Shakeel
comments.snippet: AI-powered retail footfall analytics / store pathing
comments.comment: Awais Shakeel — Footfall pathing is the insight retail teams miss when dashboards only show basket size. What signal changed your layout decisions: heatmaps vs queue dwell time?

comments.author: Gabriel Klingman
comments.snippet: ChatGPT sites underwhelmed — ship imperfect vs not ship
comments.comment: Gabriel Klingman — The "ship imperfect then not ship" take is fair, but PM teams lose trust fast when the demo path diverges from production evals. What gap did you see first: authoring UX, hosting isolation, or model routing?

comments.author: venkatesh vooturi
comments.snippet: Ferrari vs seatbelt — wealth apps optimized for ideal user
comments.comment: venkatesh vooturi — The Ferrari vs seatbelt line nails wealth UX: teams ship power-user flows while stressed users need guardrails first. Where did you narrow scope — onboarding copy or default portfolio views?

comments.author: Labbrand Group
comments.snippet: AI as voice of brand — product copy, campaigns, customer responses
comments.comment: Labbrand Group — When AI writes product copy and campaigns, PMs inherit a new failure mode: inconsistent promises across touchpoints. How are you governing tone and claims across AI-generated outputs?

comments.author: Chandrahas Kumar
comments.snippet: AI tool from calendar dread — weekly metrics across 5 tools
comments.comment: Chandrahas Kumar — The calendar-dread origin story is the PM move most teams skip before bolting on LLMs. What was the first metric you automated across those five tools before any model layer?

comments.author: Viren Oswall
comments.snippet: Promoted Senior Manager Swiggy — careers built on others' belief
comments.comment: Viren Oswall — The line about careers built on other people's belief hit — promotions rarely flip the work, they reveal who already trusted your judgment on messy tradeoffs. What did your manager actually bet on before the title changed?

comments.author: Ankit Shukla
comments.snippet: AI PM vocabulary — Wrapper to Harness, Prompt rename
comments.comment: Ankit Shukla — Renaming Wrapper to Harness is more than vocabulary — it changes how teams gate agentic ships. Which term swap has actually altered your review checklist, not just the slide deck?

comments.author: Shruti Verma
comments.snippet: Career pivot consulting→product→psychology; fear of audience judgment
comments.comment: Shruti Verma — Imagining the audience before you have the draft is the trap I see PMs hit when they pivot too — the product muscle is deciding what to cut, not what to impress. What made you publish before the psychology chapter felt "ready"?

comments.author: Kevin Thomas
comments.snippet: AI split PMs — who code vs who do not want to code
comments.comment: Kevin Thomas — The "do not want to code" framing is sharper than "cannot" — I've seen PMs lose spec credibility when they can't read a PR diff. Where do you draw the line: prototyping in prod vs validating with eng?

comments.author: Vik Gambhir
comments.snippet: Publications picked up post — year of 3-4x daily posting
comments.comment: Vik Gambhir — The gap between "picked up this week" and a year of 3–4 daily posts is the lesson PMs miss when they chase viral launches over compounding loops. What signal told you to keep volume up before any publication bit?

comments.author: Rakesh Talla
comments.snippet: US hours from India — wind down, sleep, health breakdown
comments.comment: Rakesh Talla — The "two hours to wind down" line is what teams gloss over when they celebrate global collaboration. I've seen PMs ship on US hours but lose decision quality by month three. What boundary actually stuck — meeting caps or async-first design?

comments.author: Ant Murphy
comments.snippet: Product Discovery exploring vs exploiting modes
comments.comment: Ant Murphy — The exploring vs exploiting split is the roadmap fight I see most: teams optimize flows they already understand and call it discovery. What signal do you use to know you've actually shifted into explore mode?

comments.author: Abhinav Bhatt
comments.snippet: Joined Booking.com PM from Razorpay — B2C to travel platform
comments.comment: Abhinav Bhatt — Razorpay to Booking is a sharp B2C→travel-platform shift. What changed faster than you expected in week one — inventory discovery or stakeholder depth?

comments.author: Jagriti Pasricha
comments.snippet: Matiks streaks replaced Snapchat — new position
comments.comment: Jagriti Pasricha — Replacing Snapchat streaks with Matiks streaks is a sharp habit-loop metaphor — the PM question is what daily action you wanted users to repeat beyond opening the app. What behavior earns the streak?

comments.author: Yeshwanth Reddy
comments.snippet: Director, Product at InCred Financial Services — new position
comments.comment: Yeshwanth Reddy — Moving to Director, Product at InCred is a sharp FinTech step — lending product decisions compound fast when underwriting and member UX sit in different tribes. What tradeoff did you inherit on day one: growth experiments or compliance depth?

comments.author: Aditya Pandey
comments.snippet: MTS at SurveySparrow — intern to full-time product builder
comments.comment: Aditya Pandey — The MTS jump at SurveySparrow from intern is a builder arc — the skill that transfers fastest is scoping customer pain into bets, not just shipping features. What was the first project where you owned the tradeoff, not just the implementation?

comments.author: Archit Goyal
comments.snippet: Repost Pushpak — building good products still hard with vibe coding
comments.comment: Archit Goyal — Sharing Pushpak's "building good products is still hard" take is the right PM signal — vibe-coding speeds drafts but not decision quality. What broke first on your read: eval discipline or scope boundaries?

comments.author: Dhruv Maheshwari
comments.snippet: Digi Yatra — 10 min airport flow without check-in baggage
comments.comment: Dhruv Maheshwari — The "~10 minutes with no check-in baggage" bar is the product test — Digi Yatra wins when the happy path is invisible, not when the feature list is long. What drop-off are you watching beyond adoption headlines?

comments.author: Akanksha Pandey
comments.snippet: Software Engineer JL3 at A.P. Moller - Maersk — new chapter
comments.comment: Akanksha Pandey — The SAP → Maersk move is a sharp domain shift — logistics software rewards people who can translate ops constraints into backlog clarity fast. What was the first system boundary you had to learn at Maersk vs SAP Labs?

comments.author: Hina Arora
comments.snippet: AI Agents vs Agentic AI — employee task vs planning system
comments.comment: Hina Arora — "Everyone talks AI Agents, few talk Agentic AI" is the distinction PM teams need before bolting agents onto brittle workflows. The employee-vs-system framing is crisp — where do you draw the line for human approval on multi-step plans?

comments.author: Sanchit Goyal
comments.snippet: Trying to build something — biggest problem of my life
comments.comment: Sanchit Goyal — Admitting you're "trying to build something like this" on a public post is the builder move most PMs avoid — the problem-solution fit line is honest. What user moment made you stop lurking and start building?

comments.author: Ashwin Prasad
comments.snippet: Prompt to production — non-technical users, Claude/Codex to prod apps
comments.comment: Ashwin Prasad — Targeting non-technical Claude/Codex users for production-ready apps is the right wedge — the failure mode I see is teams skipping eval gates because the demo path feels "done." What will you enforce in batch one before anyone ships to users?

comments.author: Yoshitha Gandham
comments.snippet: Promoted to Solution Architect at Phenom from Associate SA
comments.comment: Yoshitha Gandham — Associate SA to Solution Architect at Phenom is a credibility jump — the title change usually means you're now on the hook for tradeoffs, not just delivery. What part of the promotion surprised you more: scope across customers or depth in one platform area?

comments.author: Rajeev Roy
comments.snippet: The Myth of the Brand IIM — pedigree vs operator judgment
comments.comment: Rajeev Roy — The "Brand IIM" myth framing is sharp — I see hiring panels overweight pedigree when problem-framing beats institute labels. What signal do you use to separate brand halo from operator judgment?

comments.author: Teresa Torres
comments.snippet: Blacklisted by Google — opportunity solution tree SEO / producttalk
comments.comment: Teresa Torres — The "doesn't even rank for opportunity solution tree" line is brutal proof distribution beats brilliance. When discovery traffic is zero, what do you change first: structured content, internal linking, or a different entry keyword?

comments.author: Dhrumil Barot
comments.snippet: NVIDIA CPU for agents not humans — procurement reality for SaaS
comments.comment: Dhrumil Barot — CPUs built for agents (not humans) flips procurement: buyers will benchmark latency per tool-call, not per seat. For SaaS teams you work with, what is the first workload you are moving off human-UI assumptions?

comments.author: Adarsh Panda
comments.snippet: Promoted to Lead AI and Data Solutions Engineer II (Manager) at Deloitte
comments.comment: Adarsh Panda — Lead AI and Data Solutions Engineer II at Deloitte usually means owning delivery plus people, not just models. What surprised you most in the promotion: scope across accounts or depth on a single platform?

publish_title: What separates good PMs from the ones teams actually trust?
selection_strategy: publish_day_golden_hour: home feed Sort by Top only (no content search); refresh+scroll; niche PM/builder; skip hiring/promoted
